decisions.title: AI interpretation tasks run in background with completion notifications
decisions.detail: Each AI interpretation run creates one task that executes in the background within the task list, similar to the CC platform, and sends a notification upon completion.

evidence.quote: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: AI Interpretation Work Item

decisions.title: AI interpretation notification mechanism details pending confirmation
decisions.detail: The specific details of the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion need to be confirmed in a requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: AI Interpretation Work Item

decisions.title: Two image sources for AI interpretation
decisions.detail: Imagery for AI interpretation can come from two sources: a one-time, large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user.

evidence.quote: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: AI Interpretation Work Item

decisions.title: User interaction with AI interpretation results
decisions.detail: After AI interpretation completes and a list is generated, the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring.

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: AI Interpretation Work Item

decisions.title: Requirements for placing a point under monitoring
decisions.detail: When a user places a point under monitoring, they must provide a reason and upload a polygon. Large-area polygons are valid, but overly small areas will be automatically filtered out.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: AI Interpretation Work Item

decisions.title: Duplicate image upload prevention mechanism required
decisions.detail: A mechanism is needed to prevent images from being uploaded more than once, due to their large file sizes.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: AI Interpretation Work Item

decisions.title: Two-period image comparison will be manual, not from image library
decisions.detail: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull images from the image library; instead, users will annotate this manually.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Image Comparison Feature Adjustment

decisions.title: On-site workstation for model execution
decisions.detail: A workstation will be set up on-site, to which images are sent via the client's AP, and the workstation will run the model.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Image Comparison Feature Adjustment

decisions.title: Base map provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer
decisions.detail: The base map for the system will be sourced from GIS Provider B through GeoServer.

evidence.quote: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Image Comparison Feature Adjustment

decisions.title: Map viewer functionality for historical imagery and filtering
decisions.detail: The map viewer must be able to load historically uploaded imagery and support filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (with types: satellite, aerial photo, drone), while also displaying points on the map.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map viewer 'locate' button functionality
decisions.detail: When a point is opened in the map viewer, a 'locate' button should be displayed, which, when clicked, automatically zooms the map to that specific location.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Map view reverts to point display at certain zoom level
decisions.detail: When the map is zoomed out beyond a specified scale, the displayed imagery should automatically revert to a point display.

evidence.quote: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

decisions.title: Map view behavior in comparison mode
decisions.detail: In comparison mode, the map view will be locked and panning will be disabled. A 'release drag mode' button will be provided to restore panning functionality.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: Image transparency adjustment in map viewer
decisions.detail: Users must be able to adjust image transparency, which is identified as an important feature for orthophoto analysis.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Map viewer design references existing system
decisions.detail: The design of the map viewer should reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

evidence.quote: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

decisions.title: Project and suspected point organization
decisions.detail: Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within these projects.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Confirmation needed for splitting suspected point layers by county/city
decisions.detail: A decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation in a requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Suspected points require polygon upload, no image upload
decisions.detail: Suspected points must have an uploaded polygon, but do not require an image upload.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: Each year forms one suspected point layer
decisions.detail: For suspected point management, each year will form a distinct layer.

evidence.quote: Each year forms one layer

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data interface needed
decisions.detail: A 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be added, including fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

decisions.title: Fields for New Survey Point Data interface
decisions.detail: The 'New Survey Point Data' interface must include fields such as survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.

evidence.quote: [Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]

decisions.title: Migration of legacy MS file management system
decisions.detail: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system. Its interface will be redesigned, but its core functionality will remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Legacy MIS System Migration

decisions.title: Reason for MIS migration: Information security compliance
decisions.detail: The primary motivation for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements.

evidence.quote: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Legacy MIS System Migration

decisions.title: Legacy MIS system migration details pending confirmation
decisions.detail: Further details regarding the migration of the legacy MIS system are pending confirmation in a requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Legacy MIS System Migration

decisions.title: New follow-up feature integrates suspected points
decisions.detail: The planning for the new system's follow-up feature includes selecting items to follow up on from suspected points and integrating them into the follow-up module.

evidence.quote: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

decisions.title: Add schedule-tracking interface for follow-ups
decisions.detail: The new system's follow-up feature should include a schedule-tracking interface, for example, to show days remaining.

evidence.quote: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

decisions.title: Follow-up dispatch interface to be developed
decisions.detail: A follow-up dispatch interface needs to be implemented for the new system, with Shao-Chien already having a first draft.

evidence.quote: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

decisions.title: Follow-up feature connects to survey-report management
decisions.detail: After a follow-up action, the system should connect to survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

evidence.quote: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

decisions.title: A-Bao requires data-flow documentation
decisions.detail: A-Bao has requested a data-flow document to explain how data moves after AI interpretation completes, specifically where data is pulled from and where computation occurs.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)

decisions.title: Defined data flow for AI interpretation
decisions.detail: The data flow for AI interpretation covers the path from the image source, through interpretation task execution (at the workstation), to result output, and finally to the monitoring database.

evidence.quote: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)

decisions.title: Project kick-off expected mid-May if bidding successful
decisions.detail: The project is currently in the bidding process and, if it goes smoothly, is expected to kick off in mid-May.

evidence.quote: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

decisions.title: Potential immediate requirements interview post-bid award
decisions.detail: There is a possibility that a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to occur immediately on the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, indicating a tight schedule.

evidence.quote: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

decisions.title: Next meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15 to discuss system architecture
decisions.detail: The next meeting is scheduled for May 15, 2026, with the planned topic of discussing system architecture.

decisions.detail: The first requirements interview is scheduled for May 20, 2026.

decisions.detail: Preparation for the requirements interview includes consolidating existing interfaces, preparing a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram.

decisions.detail: The details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism are an open item that needs confirmation.

decisions.detail: It is an open question whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city.

decisions.detail: The interface design details for the MIS migration are an open item requiring confirmation.

decisions.detail: Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule is an open item needing confirmation.

implementation_scope: Implement background task processing for AI interpretation runs, including task list integration (similar to CC platform) and completion notifications.
Develop AI interpretation image ingestion mechanisms from two sources: Vendor A nationwide batch and individual user uploads.
Develop user interface and backend logic for confirming AI interpretation suspected points (yes/no) and assigning them to 'watch/monitoring'.
Implement logic for placing points under monitoring, requiring a reason and polygon upload, with automated filtering for overly small polygons.
Implement a duplicate image upload prevention mechanism for large image files.
Develop a map viewer component capable of loading historically uploaded imagery.
Implement map viewer filtering functionality by date and image-name keyword (supporting types: satellite, aerial photo, drone).
Implement map viewer functionality to display points on the map.
Implement a 'locate' button in the map viewer to automatically zoom to a specific point's location.
Implement map viewer logic to revert to point display when zoomed out beyond a specified scale.
Implement comparison mode for the map viewer, including locking panning and providing a 'release drag mode' button.
Implement image transparency adjustment functionality in the map viewer.
Implement project organization by year and allow creation of suspected points within these annual projects.
Implement suspected point creation, requiring polygon upload and no image upload.
Ensure each year forms a distinct suspected point layer in management.

Develop a 'New Survey Point Data' interface with fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.
Migrate the legacy MS file management system to the new system, redesigning the interface while retaining core functionality.
Implement a new follow-up feature, allowing selection of suspected points for follow-up and integration into a follow-up module.
Develop a schedule-tracking interface for follow-ups (e.g., showing days remaining).
Implement a follow-up dispatch interface.
Integrate follow-up actions with survey-report management, recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

constraints.text: AI interpretation model execution must occur on an on-site workstation, receiving images via the client's AP.
The base map must be sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The map viewer design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The legacy MIS system migration is primarily driven by information-security compliance requirements.
The defined data flow for AI interpretation must follow the path: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database.
The project is expected to kick off in mid-May if bidding is successful, implying a tight schedule.
There is a risk of an immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation on the afternoon of bid award, requiring rapid preparation.
The 'add suspected point' data interface, referencing manual figure 2.13, needs to be completed by 2026-05-18.
Existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) must be organized for the requirements interview by 2026-05-20.
A data-flow document (covering image source, compute node, and data direction) must be prepared for A-Bao's review by 2026-05-18.
System architecture discussion meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15.
Preparation for the requirements interview requires consolidating existing interfaces, a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram.

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature will not utilize the image library; manual user annotation is the chosen approach.
The legacy MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, requiring interface redesign while maintaining core functionality.

open_technical_questions: What are the specific implementation details for the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., protocol, payload, endpoints)? (D002, D039)
Should suspected point layers be logically or physically split by county/city, impacting data modeling and GIS query performance? (D017, D040)
What are the detailed interface designs (e.g., API contracts, data structures) for the MIS migration? (D041)
What are the technical requirements for recipients and scheduling of follow-up notifications, impacting notification service implementation and user preference management? (D042)

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation task is initiated, When the task starts processing, Then it appears in the background task list.
Given an AI interpretation task is initiated, When the task completes successfully, Then a completion notification is sent.
Given a user initiates AI interpretation, When selecting image source, Then 'Vendor A Nationwide Batch' and 'User Uploaded Individual Images' are available options.
Given AI interpretation has completed and results are displayed, When a list of suspected points is presented, Then the user can confirm a suspected point (Yes/No).
Given a user confirms a suspected point, When the user decides to place it under monitoring, Then the system allows this action.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user submits the monitoring request, Then a reason must be provided.
Given a user attempts to place a suspected point under monitoring, When the user submits the monitoring request, Then a polygon must be uploaded.
Given a user uploads a polygon for monitoring, When the polygon is of a large area, Then the system accepts it.
Given a user uploads a polygon for monitoring, When the polygon is an overly small area, Then the system filters it out automatically.
Given a user attempts to upload an image, When the image has already been uploaded previously, Then the system prevents the duplicate upload.
Given the two-period image comparison feature is accessed, When the user attempts to retrieve images, Then the system does not pull images from the image library.
Given the two-period image comparison feature is accessed, When the user needs to compare images, Then the user can manually annotate the comparison.
Given the map viewer is displayed, When the base map loads, Then it is sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
Given the map viewer is displayed, When historical imagery has been uploaded, Then the map viewer loads and displays it.
Given historical imagery is loaded, When the user filters by date, Then only imagery matching the date filter is displayed.
Given historical imagery is loaded, When the user filters by image-name keyword, Then only imagery matching the keyword is displayed.
Given historical imagery is loaded, When the user filters by image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone), Then only imagery of the selected type is displayed.
Given the map viewer is displayed, When suspected points exist, Then they are displayed on the map.
Given a point is selected in the map viewer, When a 'locate' button is displayed for that point, Then clicking the 'locate' button automatically zooms the map to that point's location.
Given the map viewer is displaying imagery, When the map is zoomed out beyond a specified scale, Then the displayed imagery automatically reverts to a point display.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When the user attempts to pan the map, Then panning is disabled.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When panning is disabled, Then a 'release drag mode' button is visible.
Given the map viewer is in comparison mode, When the 'release drag mode' button is clicked, Then panning functionality is restored.
Given an image is displayed in the map viewer, When the user adjusts the transparency control, Then the image's transparency changes accordingly.
Given a user is creating a new project, When the project is created, Then it is associated with a specific year.
Given a project exists, When a user creates a suspected point, Then the suspected point can be created within that project.
Given a user is creating a suspected point, When the user attempts to save the suspected point, Then a polygon must be uploaded.
Given a user is creating a suspected point, When the user attempts to save the suspected point, Then an image upload is not required.
Given suspected points exist across multiple years, When viewing suspected points, Then each year's suspected points are presented as a distinct layer.
Given the system is accessed, When the user navigates to create new survey point data, Then a 'New Survey Point Data' interface is displayed.
Given the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is displayed, When data entry is required, Then fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone' are present.

Given the 'New Survey Point Data' interface is displayed, When data entry is required, Then the following fields are present: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.
Given the legacy MS file management system existed, When the new system is used, Then its core functionality is present and unchanged.
Given the legacy MS file management system existed, When the new system is used, Then its interface is redesigned.
Given suspected points exist, When the user creates a follow-up item, Then suspected points can be selected and added to the follow-up module.
Given a follow-up item is being tracked, When the follow-up module is accessed, Then a schedule-tracking interface is displayed.
Given a schedule-tracking interface is displayed, When a follow-up item has a deadline, Then the interface shows days remaining for that item.
Given the system implements follow-up functionality, When a user needs to dispatch a follow-up, Then a follow-up dispatch interface is available.
Given a follow-up action is completed, When the system processes the completion, Then it records the action's time, description, action taken, and any uploaded images in survey-report management.
test_scenarios: Initiate an AI interpretation task and verify it appears in the background task list and a completion notification is received upon success.
Initiate AI interpretation using a Vendor A batch image source.
Initiate AI interpretation using a user-uploaded individual image source.
After AI interpretation completes, confirm a suspected point ('Yes') and verify the option to place it under monitoring.
After AI interpretation completes, mark a suspected point as 'No' (not confirmed).
Successfully place a suspected point under monitoring by providing a reason and uploading a large-area polygon.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without providing a reason and verify the system prevents the action.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without uploading a polygon and verify the system prevents the action.
Upload an image, then attempt to upload the exact same image again and verify the duplicate upload is prevented.
Verify the image library is not accessible or used for two-period image comparison, and manual annotation tools are available.
Load the map viewer and verify the base map from GIS Provider B via GeoServer displays correctly.
Upload historical imagery and verify it loads and displays in the map viewer.
Filter historical imagery by date range, image-name keyword, and different image types (satellite, aerial photo, drone) and verify accurate results.
Verify suspected points appear correctly on the map.
Select a point on the map, click the 'locate' button, and verify the map automatically zooms to that location.
Zoom out the map beyond the specified scale and verify imagery reverts to point display; then zoom back in to verify imagery reappears.
Enter comparison mode, attempt to pan the map and verify it's locked. Click 'release drag mode' and verify panning is restored.
Load an image in the map viewer and adjust its transparency using the control, verifying visual changes.
Create a project for the current year and create a suspected point within it.
Create suspected points in different years (e.g., Year A and Year B) and verify they are organized into distinct layers.
Create a suspected point, upload a polygon, and verify successful creation without requiring an image upload.
Attempt to create a suspected point without uploading a polygon and verify an error message is displayed.

Navigate to the 'New Survey Point Data' interface and verify the presence of all specified fields (survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location).
Perform common tasks using the migrated MIS file management system's new interface and verify core functionality remains unchanged.
Create a suspected point, then select it and integrate it into a new follow-up item.
Create a follow-up item with a deadline and verify the schedule-tracking interface accurately displays 'days remaining'.
Navigate to the follow-up dispatch interface and verify its accessibility.
Complete a follow-up action, providing time, description, action taken, and uploading an image, then verify these details are correctly recorded in survey-report management.
edge_cases: What happens if the AI interpretation task fails or is cancelled?
What happens if Vendor A batch imagery is unavailable or corrupt?
What if a user tries to upload an unsupported image format for AI interpretation?
What if the list of AI interpretation suspected points is empty or contains only one point?
Test with polygons at the boundary of 'overly small area' definition for monitoring points.
What constitutes a 'duplicate' image (e.g., hash, filename, content)?
What happens if a slightly modified version of an already uploaded image is attempted for upload?
Filter map imagery with no matching results or with invalid date formats/keywords.
Test the map zoom behavior at the exact boundary of the 'specified scale' for reverting to point display.
What if a suspected point is deleted after being integrated into a follow-up item?
What if a follow-up item has no defined deadline or if the deadline is in the past?
Test all validation rules for each field in the 'New Survey Point Data' interface (e.g., numeric values, date formats, dropdown selections).
unresolved_behaviors: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (D002, D039).
Whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city (D017, D040).
Interface design details for the MIS migration (D024, D041).
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule (D042).

decision_history: Confirmed AI interpretation tasks will run in the background with completion notifications, similar to the CC platform.
Decided AI interpretation will process imagery from two sources: a one-time nationwide batch from Vendor A and user-uploaded individual images.
Confirmed users can confirm suspected points and decide on monitoring after AI interpretation generates a list.
When placing a point under monitoring, users must provide a reason and upload a polygon, with automatic filtering for overly small areas.
A mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads is required due to large file sizes.
The two-period image comparison feature will now involve manual annotation by users, no longer pulling images from an image library.
An on-site workstation will be set up to run AI models, with images sent via the client's AP.
The base map for the system will be sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The map viewer must support loading historical imagery, filtering by date and image-name keyword (types: satellite, aerial photo, drone), and displaying points.
The map viewer will feature a 'locate' button that zooms to a specific point when clicked.
The map view will automatically revert to point display when zoomed out beyond a specified scale.
In comparison mode, the map view will be locked (panning disabled), with a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning.
Users must be able to adjust image transparency in the map viewer for orthophoto analysis.
The map viewer design should reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points created within them.
Suspected points require a polygon upload but do not require an image upload.
Each year will form a distinct suspected point layer.
A 'New Survey Point Data' interface must be added, including fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13.

The 'New Survey Point Data' interface will include fields such as survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method, volume, area, section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.

The legacy MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with redesigned interface but unchanged core functionality. The primary motivation for this migration is information security compliance requirements.
The new follow-up feature will allow selecting and integrating items to follow up on from suspected points into the follow-up module.
A schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining) will be added to the follow-up feature.
A follow-up dispatch interface needs to be implemented, with a first draft already available from Shao-Chien.
After a follow-up action, the system will connect to survey-report management, recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
The data flow for AI interpretation is defined as: image source  	 interpretation task execution (workstation)  	 result output  	 monitoring database.
dependencies: Integration with GIS Provider B via GeoServer is required for base map provision.
A data-flow document explaining AI interpretation data movement (image source, computation, data pull) is required by A-Bao for review by 2026-05-18 (Sherry assigned).
Completion of the 'add suspected point' data interface by Sherry is needed by 2026-05-18 for downstream work.
Organized existing interfaces (by Sherry, including Shao-Chien's contributions) are required by 2026-05-20 for the requirements interview.
Consolidated existing interfaces, a system architecture diagram, and a data flow diagram are needed as preparation for the first requirements interview scheduled for 2026-05-20.
risks: High Likelihood/High Impact: A requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to occur immediately on the afternoon of the bid award, indicating a very tight schedule and potential for rushed decisions or missed details.
alignment_gaps: Specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism are pending confirmation.
Whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city requires confirmation.
Interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration are pending confirmation.
Recipients and schedule for follow-up notifications are not yet confirmed.
open_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism? (PM/QA)
Should the suspected-point layer be split by county/city? (PM/QA)
What are the specific interface design details for the MIS migration? (PM/QA)
Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule? (PM/QA)
milestones: Project kick-off expected mid-May (contingent on successful bidding).
Sherry to finish 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18.
Sherry to organize existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) for requirements interview by 2026-05-20.
Sherry to prepare data-flow document for A-Bao review by 2026-05-18.
Next meeting to discuss system architecture scheduled for 2026-05-15.

system_requirements: The system SHALL execute AI interpretation runs as background tasks within a task list, similar to the CC platform.
The system SHALL send a notification upon the completion of each AI interpretation task.
The system SHALL support two sources for AI interpretation imagery: a one-time, large-scale nationwide batch from Vendor A, and individual images uploaded by the user.
Upon completion of AI interpretation, the system SHALL generate a list of suspected points.
The system SHALL enable users to confirm suspected points (Yes/No).
The system SHALL enable users to decide whether to place confirmed suspected points under watch/monitoring.
When placing a point under monitoring, the user SHALL be required to provide a reason.
When placing a point under monitoring, the user SHALL be required to upload a polygon.
The system SHALL automatically filter out overly small polygons provided for monitoring areas.
The system SHALL accept large-area polygons for monitoring purposes (inferred — needs confirmation).
The system MUST prevent the upload of duplicate image files, considering their large file sizes.
The system SHALL identify duplicate images prior to or during upload (inferred — needs confirmation).
The map viewer SHALL be able to load historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the loaded imagery list by date.
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the loaded imagery list by image-name keyword, with categories: satellite, aerial photo, and drone.
The map viewer SHALL display suspected points on the map.
When a point is selected or opened in the map viewer, the system SHALL display a 'locate' button.
Upon user activation of the 'locate' button, the map viewer SHALL automatically zoom to the location of the selected point.
When the map is zoomed out beyond a specified scale, the map viewer SHALL automatically revert the displayed imagery to a point display.
In comparison mode, the map view SHALL be locked, and panning SHALL be disabled.
The system SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button in comparison mode to restore panning functionality.
The map viewer SHALL allow users to adjust the transparency of displayed images.
The system SHALL organize projects by year.
The system SHALL allow suspected points to be created within existing projects.
When creating a suspected point, the system SHALL require the upload of a polygon.
When creating a suspected point, the system SHALL NOT require the upload of an image.
For suspected point management, each year SHALL form a distinct suspected point layer.
The system SHALL include a 'New Survey Point Data' interface.
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone'.
The 'New Survey Point Data' interface design SHALL reference manual figure 2.13 (inferred — needs confirmation).

The 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL include the following fields: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location.
The system MUST ensure compliance with information security requirements during the migration and operation of the legacy MS file management system.
The system SHALL allow users to select items for follow-up from suspected points.
The system SHALL integrate selected suspected points into the follow-up module.
The follow-up feature SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface, displaying information such as days remaining for a follow-up item.
The system SHALL implement a follow-up dispatch interface.
Following a follow-up action, the system SHALL connect to survey-report management.
The survey-report management functionality SHALL record follow-up time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
data_flows: Imagery data from Vendor A (batch) to AI Interpretation system.
Imagery data from user uploads to AI Interpretation system.
Images from client's Access Point (AP) to the on-site workstation for AI model execution.
AI interpretation results from the workstation to the monitoring database.
Base map data from GIS Provider B (via GeoServer) to Map Viewer.
interface_contracts: The 'New Survey Point Data' interface requires input fields for: survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, and approximate location. (Input specification for New Survey Point Data interface)

The Follow-up module requires integration points with Survey Report Management to record time, description, action taken, and uploaded images for follow-up actions (Integration contract for Follow-up to Survey Report Management).

constraints.text: AI interpretation models MUST be executed on a dedicated on-site workstation.
The system's base map MUST be sourced from GIS Provider B via GeoServer.
The map viewer's design MUST reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The data flow for AI interpretation MUST follow the path: image source → interpretation task execution (on workstation) → result output → monitoring database.
Project kick-off is expected in mid-May (contingent on successful bidding).
A requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required immediately on the day the project bid is awarded, implying an accelerated timeline for initial specification.

architectural_decisions.text: The two-period image comparison feature will involve manual annotation by users and will not integrate with an image library for sourcing images.
The legacy MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with a redesigned interface but unchanged core functionality.

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., notification channels, content, recipient rules)?
Should suspected point layers be split by county/city for organizational purposes within the suspected point management module?
What are the detailed interface design specifications for the migrated legacy MIS system, ensuring unchanged core functionality?
Who are the recipients of follow-up notifications, and what is the defined schedule for these notifications?

stakeholder.business_summary: This meeting clarified key features for the new monitoring system, focusing on how artificial intelligence (AI) will interpret images, improvements to the interactive map, and the necessary migration of an older system to meet security standards. These updates aim to enhance our ability to efficiently detect, monitor, and follow up on potential issues using visual data and AI. We are targeting a project kick-off in mid-May, pending successful bidding.
key_impact: AI image analysis will run automatically in the background, promptly notifying users when potential issues are detected, which should accelerate initial identification.
Users will have improved tools to review AI-identified points and place them under monitoring, streamlining the process of confirming and acting on critical findings.
The system will support flexible image input, accommodating both large nationwide datasets and individual user uploads for AI analysis.
Migrating our legacy file management system is essential to ensure compliance with current information security requirements.
The enhanced map viewer will provide better visualization of historical imagery and suspected points, aiding in comprehensive analysis and decision-making.
New follow-up features will enable more efficient tracking and reporting on identified issues, ensuring they are addressed systematically.
decisions_needing_alignment: Confirmation is needed on the specific details of how users will be notified once AI image analysis tasks are completed.
We need to finalize whether suspected point layers (areas of interest) should be organized and displayed separately by county or city.
The exact interface design details for the migration of the legacy file management system require stakeholder confirmation.
Clarity is needed on who will receive follow-up notifications and the schedule for these alerts.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the precise mechanisms and content for notifications sent when AI image interpretation is finished?
Should we categorize and display suspected points by geographic regions like counties or cities, and what are the implications of this choice?
What will the user interface for the migrated legacy file management system look like, and what are the specific design preferences?
Who are the intended recipients for follow-up notifications, and what should be the frequency or triggers for these communications?

issues.description: The data flow description uses non-standard characters ('\b\t') which appear to be rendering issues for '->'.
issues.suggestion: Replace '\b\t' with '->' for clearer readability in the data flow description.

review.reviewer_notes: All role-specific outputs are accurate, consistent, and well-aligned with the extracted decisions. No critical hallucinations or significant quality issues were found. The open questions and dependencies are correctly identified across relevant views. A minor formatting issue was noted in the PM view's data flow description.

raw_notes:
# Work Meeting Minutes

**Project:** Illegal Excavation AI — Work Item Discussion
**Date:** 2026-05-04
**Attendees:** A-Bao, Ming-Xian, Sherry
**Minutes by:** Sherry

## Topics for This Session

- Confirming the functional scope of each Illegal-Excavation-AI work item
- Migration plan for the legacy MIS system
- AI interpretation task workflow and data flow explanation
- Map viewer features and image-comparison mode discussion
- Follow-up/reminder module planning

## Decisions

### [AI Interpretation Work Item]

- Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes
- Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview
- Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user
- After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring
- When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)
- Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

### [Image Comparison Feature Adjustment]

- Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead
- A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model
- The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

### [Map Viewer Features]

- The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map
- Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location
- When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display
- In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning
- Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)
- The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

### [Suspected Point Management]

- Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project
- Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview
- Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed
- Each year forms one layer
- **Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)**

  *[Screenshot: "New Survey Point Data" form, before/after clicking "Edit" — fields include survey serial number, drawing number, key monitoring zone, village, monitoring date, monitoring method (precise coordinates / aerial survey / rapid monitoring / unmanned equipment), volume (cubic meters), area (square meters), section, lot number, X/Y coordinates, longitude/latitude, approximate location]*

### [Legacy MIS System Migration]

- The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged
- The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements
- Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

### [Follow-up/Reminder Feature]

- The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification
- Planning for the new system's follow-up feature:
  - Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module
  - Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)
  - Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)
  - After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

### [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

- A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
- Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database

### [Project Timeline]

- The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May
- On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

## Action Items

| Owner | Task | Due |
|---|---|---|
| Sherry | Finish the "add suspected point" data interface (refer to figure 2.13) | Before 5/18 |
| Sherry | Organize existing interfaces (including the parts Shao-Chien owns) for use in the requirements interview | Before 5/20 |
| Sherry | Prepare the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review | Before 5/18 |

## Next Meeting

**Time:** 2026-05-15
**Planned topic:** Discuss system architecture

## Requirements Interview

**Time:** 2026-05-20
**Planned topic:** First requirements interview
**Prep needed:** Consolidate existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, data flow diagram

**Open items to confirm:**
- Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism
- Whether the suspected-point layer should be split by county/city
- Interface design details for the MIS migration
- Who receives follow-up notifications and on what schedule
